svg.text: Mega-Fused Attention: RMSNorm → QKV → RoPE → Flash Attention
Single DRAM Read → All Compute in Cache → Single DRAM Write
The Problem: Unfused Attention Memory Traffic

g.text: L3 Cache: W_q, W_k, W_v, W_o weight tiles (stream through)
Weights too large → tile and stream, reuse per seq tile
L2 Cache: Q, K, V tiles + KV Cache slice
Working set: Q_tile[B_q, H] + K_tile[B_k, H] + V_tile[B_k, H]
L1 Cache: GEMM tiles + RoPE cos/sin
64×64 matmul tiles + position embeddings for current tile
for each K_tile, V_tile from KV cache:
m_new, l_new, O_tile = online_softmax_update(S_ij, V_tile, m_i, l_i, O_tile)

svg.text: Memory Traffic Analysis (Decode: seq=1, hidden=2048, heads=32)
// Mega-fused attention kernel for decode (single token)
float* out, float* x, float* W_qkv, float* W_o,
float* kv_cache, float* rope_cos, float* rope_sin,
int pos, int H, int n_heads, int head_dim) {
// Step 4: Flash Attention with online softmax
flash_attention_tile(O, m, l, Q, kv_cache, t);
// Process each KV tile without materializing full S matrix
S_ij = Q_tile @ K_tile.T / sqrt(d) // [B_q × B_k] in registers
Key insight: O, m, l stay in registers across ALL KV tiles!

g.text: • Q,K,V stay in L2, RoPE in registers
• Tile Q×K^T to fit in registers

svg.text: Fused Bytes = H×2 (input) + H×2 (output) = 4×H bytes (decode mode)
AI_fused ≈ H + S FLOPs/byte → For H=2048, S=2048: AI ≈ 4096 FLOPs/byte (MASSIVELY compute-bound!)
